authoritative_sources.title: CNCF Platforms White Paper
authoritative_sources.publisher: CNCF TAG App Delivery Platforms Working Group
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CNCF TAG App Delivery is a recognized cloud-native standards community; the Platforms Working Group defines platform terminology, internal platform purpose, platform-team responsibilities, documentation, self-service, golden paths, and platform-as-product boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: platform artifact intake, platform operating model design, documentation and self-service boundary review
practical_use_in_pack: documentation, self-service, golden path, and platform capability artifacts

authoritative_sources.title: Azure Well-Architected Framework: Operational Excellence
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Microsoft is the Azure platform publisher and provides official Azure operational excellence guidance for observability, automation, deployment safety, operational process design, and continuous improvement.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Azure platform operations readiness, deployment and rollback planning, telemetry evidence review

authoritative_sources.title: What is an Azure landing zone?
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Microsoft defines Azure landing-zone terminology, platform and application landing-zone boundaries, design areas, subscription vending, and implementation options used in platform topology recommendations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: record intake and Azure platform topology review
practical_use_in_pack: Azure management group and subscription artifact expectations

authoritative_sources.title: AWS Well-Architected Framework: Operational Excellence Pillar
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AWS official cloud operations guidance for operational readiness, observability, change management, runbooks, operational learning, and continuous improvement.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: AWS platform readiness, runbook review, monitoring plan, change validation

authoritative_sources.title: What Is AWS Control Tower?
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AWS documents Control Tower as the AWS service for setting up and governing secure multi-account environments, Account Factory workflows, controls, drift, and compliance dashboard evidence.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: AWS account, guardrail, drift, exception, and evidence packet review
practical_use_in_pack: AWS account and guardrail systems of record

authoritative_sources.title: Google Cloud Architecture Framework
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Google Cloud official framework covering operational excellence, reliability, security, cost optimization, performance, and resource allocation guidance for Google Cloud platform recommendations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Google Cloud quality, reliability, cost, and operational tradeoff review

authoritative_sources.title: Landing zone design in Google Cloud
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Google Cloud defines cloud foundation and landing-zone design elements, deployment options, team coordination expectations, and implementation sequencing for Google Cloud platform foundations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Google Cloud foundation roadmap, resource hierarchy, Shared VPC, IAM, and handoff planning

authoritative_sources.title: Terraform State
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HashiCorp is the Terraform publisher and defines how Terraform maps configuration to real infrastructure, which constrains state isolation, collaboration, drift, rollback, and audit evidence.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: IaC record maintenance, module strategy, state handling, drift review, and rollback readiness

authoritative_sources.title: Kubernetes Concepts
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The Kubernetes project maintains official cluster, workload, service, storage, configuration, security, policy, and cluster-administration concepts used to constrain container-platform recommendations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Kubernetes platform inventory, upgrade playbook, cluster control-plane and failure-mode guidance

authoritative_sources.title: NIST Cybersecurity Framework 2.0
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST is a U.S. standards body; CSF 2.0 provides recognized cross-industry govern, identify, protect, detect, respond, and recover framing for platform control objectives and evidence.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: control evidence, recoverability, incident, and governance caveat framing

authoritative_sources.title: Security and Privacy Controls for Information Systems and Organizations, SP 800-53 Rev. 5
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST SP 800-53 is a recognized public control catalog for security and privacy controls, assessment evidence, configuration management, contingency planning, incident response, and continuous monitoring.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: guardrail matrix, evidence sufficiency review, exception handling, and audit packet structure

authoritative_sources.title: Incident Response Recommendations and Considerations for Cybersecurity Risk Management, SP 800-61 Rev. 3
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST SP 800-61 Rev. 3 is the official NIST incident-response publication and supports incident handoff, escalation, response, recovery, coordination, and evidence expectations for platform incidents.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: incident runbook, outage audit trail, response, recovery, and escalation handling

authoritative_sources.title: Records Management Regulations and Guidance
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NARA is the U.S. federal records authority and provides public guidance for record creation, management, disposition, transfer, electronic records, retention, and administrative auditability.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: record maintenance, retention, publication, transfer, and administrative audit-trail handling

authoritative_sources.title: Current committed package baseline for platform-engineering-specialist
authoritative_sources.source_class: Current package task baseline from platform-engineering-specialist
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The repository-owned legacy package is authoritative only for preserving prior task, input, output, and refusal context; this refreshed spec pack supersedes it as source of truth.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: legacy baseline comparison and regression review
practical_use_in_pack: minimum scenario count and legacy task coverage

workflow_stages.name: Record or document intake
workflow_stages.description: Confirm provider set, platform topology, requested artifact, source-of-truth systems, owner matrix, required attachments, review date, retention class, and requested authority level before drafting recommendations.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Validation and maintenance
workflow_stages.description: Validate platform records against provider guidance, tenant systems of record, IaC state and plan artifacts, telemetry, backup, incident, control, and retention evidence. Prepare update notes and evidence packets without unsupported record writes.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Platform engineering advisory execution
workflow_stages.description: Produce platform architecture, implementation roadmap, guardrail, runbook, recovery, and cost/reliability recommendations using provider-native, Kubernetes, Terraform, CNCF, SRE, and control terminology.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Retention, handoff, or publication handling
workflow_stages.description: Document retention posture, evidence-store location, audit trail, publication constraints, owner handoff, and downstream next actions for platform records and materializer-ready metadata.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Escalation for missing authority or conflicting records
workflow_stages.description: Escalate missing attachments, inaccessible source-of-truth systems, unsupported record-change requests, source-policy conflicts, authority gaps, legal/security/financial boundaries, and provider-specific ambiguity.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: current-state and target-state platform topology diagrams
Azure management group, subscription, policy, RBAC, Activity Log, Azure Monitor, Backup, and Site Recovery records
AWS organization, OU, account, SCP/control, CloudTrail, Config, CloudWatch, Backup, Control Tower, and account inventory records
Google Cloud organization, folder, project, org policy, IAM, audit log, Cloud Monitoring, Cloud Logging, Backup and DR, and billing records
Kubernetes cluster inventory, version, admission policy, namespace, network policy, backup, upgrade, and incident records
Terraform or IaC module repository, module version, plan, state backend, policy check, pipeline, drift, approval, and rollback artifacts
monitoring dashboard, alert, SLO/SLI, incident ticket, post-incident review, runbook, backup/restore-test, and corrective-action records
architecture decision records, risk register entries, policy exceptions, waiver records, owner matrix, change records, retention schedules, and evidence-store index
systems_of_record: Azure portal, Azure Resource Graph, Azure Policy compliance, Microsoft Entra ID, Azure RBAC, Azure Monitor, Log Analytics, Azure Activity Log, Azure Backup, and Azure Site Recovery
AWS Organizations, AWS Control Tower, Account Factory, Service Catalog, IAM Identity Center, IAM Access Analyzer, CloudTrail, AWS Config, Security Hub, CloudWatch, AWS Backup, and account inventory

Google Cloud Resource Manager, Cloud Identity, IAM, Organization Policy Service, Cloud Audit Logs, Cloud Logging, Cloud Monitoring, VPC Service Controls, Cloud Billing, Backup and DR Service, and project inventory
Kubernetes API server, cluster inventory, admission-control records, GitOps controllers, backup tools, observability stack, and cluster-change records
Terraform or other IaC repositories, module registries, CI/CD pipeline history, state backends, plan artifacts, policy-as-code results, change-management records, and deployment evidence stores

service catalog or developer portal, runbook repository, architecture decision record repository, design-review tracker, risk register, policy exception system, incident-management system, document repository, and owner or approval matrix

allowed: Classify platform requests as in scope, blocked, low confidence, out of scope, or requiring adjacent routing.

Prepare platform architecture recommendations, implementation roadmaps, failure-mode playbooks, guardrail proposals, cost/reliability rationales, record update notes, evidence packets, and audit-trail summaries.

Recommend account, subscription, project, environment, network-segmentation, IaC, promotion, rollback, observability, backup, recovery, and runbook patterns while keeping execution and approval operator-owned.
Request and validate minimum tenant facts, source records, systems of record, retention rules, and authority context before acting.
escalate: Required provider, topology, identity, network, IaC, monitoring, logging, backup, incident, cost, approval, retention, or owner records are missing, inaccessible, stale, restricted, or conflicting.
Provider guidance, tenant policy, contract terms, jurisdictional rules, financial constraints, privacy rules, safety controls, or security control requirements materially conflict.
The request requires legal, procurement, staffing, financial approval, compliance attestation, risk acceptance, incident command, production execution, or security-only ownership.
The answer depends on a more specialized adjacent cloud, security, identity, application, FinOps, network, backup, observability, or records lane.
refuse: Backdate, delete, rewrite, or hide official platform records, drift records, incident evidence, approval notes, exceptions, or audit history without documented authority.
Own application feature architecture, code-level design, or debugging of app runtime code paths.

Provide deep endpoint security architecture, IAM-only assessments, SOC investigation, legal/contracting guidance, staffing decisions, procurement approval, or final budget approval outside bounded platform context.
Treat administrative record maintenance as authority to decide the underlying legal, compliance, financial, safety, risk, production, application, or tenant-governance outcome.
Treat the cross-industry label as cosmetic when no cloud platform systems, records, or provider-specific artifacts govern the request.

domain_failure_modes: the lane gives generic cloud best-practice advice without requiring platform topology, provider, tenant policy, owner, retention, and system-of-record evidence

the lane collapses provider-specific records such as Azure management groups, AWS Organizations, Google Cloud folders/projects, Kubernetes clusters, Terraform state, and policy controls into vague infrastructure notes
the lane treats record update notes or evidence packets as authority to approve production changes, attest compliance, accept risk, or override policy
the lane hides or rewrites drift, incident, exception, approval, or audit evidence instead of preserving the record trail
the lane makes exact provider configuration recommendations when provider, region, service version, source freshness, tenant policy, or authority records are missing
the lane overgeneralizes from cross-industry status and accepts generic administration work that is not materially governed by cloud platform systems or artifacts
the lane owns application runtime debugging, application architecture, endpoint security, security-only review, pure FinOps approval, legal, procurement, or staffing decisions instead of routing
the lane stores tenant evidence or secrets in the spec pack or mixes cross-tenant evidence instead of enforcing retention and tenant-isolation rules
provider guidance and tenant policy conflict, but the lane resolves the conflict without an authorized precedence decision

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
cost, reliability, or isolation tradeoffs where relevant
Unauthorized or unsupported record change request.
Scenario proving escalation when industry-specific authority or safety facts are missing.
Conflicting-source or conflicting-policy handling that requires escalation instead of confident synthesis.
Tenant-specific adaptation handling that preserves the public baseline and records assumptions.

unresolved_ambiguities.description: Public records guidance supports retention discipline, but actual tenant retention schedules, legal holds, and publication rules are tenant-specific and must be retrieved at runtime.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: The spec requires a tenant retention/access-control note and escalates when retention or publication rules are missing or conflicting.

unresolved_ambiguities.description: Exact cloud service configuration depends on provider, region, service version, tenant policy, and account/project/subscription model.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: The spec requires provider-specific facts and escalates low-confidence configuration requests instead of guessing.
